reasoning: 386 hanabi entries returned but nearly all lack prefecture, coordinates, and English descriptions — bulk names are Japanese-only. RULE C: data is authoritative (Wikipedia/Wikidata) but scope gap (no English translations, no dates, no location for most entries) makes the response very hard for an English-query agent to use practically.
missing_data: Dates/months for each festival, English names for most entries, coordinates for most entries, viewing tips or crowd info.
improvement_hint: Pre-filter to entries with coordinates + add month/date metadata; ensure English name field is populated for hanabi entries before surfacing them.

reasoning: Same dataset as L3-01 returned in Japanese (lang=ja matches query language — RULE D satisfied). 386 entries but most lack prefecture, coordinates, and dates. Authoritative source (RULE C) but practical usefulness low due to missing location/date fields.
missing_data: Dates, coordinates for most entries, viewing locations within the festival area.
improvement_hint: Enrich hanabi records with date_month, coordinates, and location strings before exposing in get_festivals.

reasoning: Query asks for snow festivals in Japan (Hokkaido is a valid geographic narrowing). Response returns 0 festival items — the festivals index has no snow-festival entries for Hokkaido. The national_heritage fallback shows UNESCO ICH items irrelevant to snow festivals. Retrieval failed entirely for the requested category.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, other yuki-matsuri entries — all absent from the index.
improvement_hint: Add yuki_matsuri kind tag and seed snow festival entries (Sapporo, Asahikawa) into the festivals corpus so Hokkaido queries return relevant results.

reasoning: RULE A: wrong tool called (get_japan_heritage for rural-life-experience query). Response returns heritage buildings (teahouses, castles, archaeological sites) — none directly address 田舎暮らし体験 (rural living experience). Authoritative data but entirely wrong class. Score 3 not 2 per RULE C for the data quality; dim hit on intent.
missing_data: Nōsōn-taiken (rural stay/farming experience) facilities, agritourism operators, satoyama destinations.
improvement_hint: Rural-life-experience queries should route to search_area with q=農家体験 or 田舎暮らし; get_japan_heritage is not the right tool.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Korean (ko) but results have no Korean name/description fields — entries show name_ja and name_en only. One dim penalty applied to constraint_handling. Data itself is real (Momijidani, Kenroku-en, Himeji Castle) with correct heritage tags and some coordinates. Useful but language mismatch for ko user.
missing_data: Korean (name_ko, description_ko) fields for the returned spots; peak-foliage timing data.
improvement_hint: Ensure Korean translation fields are populated for key sakura_meisho_100/紅葉 spots so ko-language queries get localized output.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet, non-touristy meditation places, not touristy temples. Response returns 修行 (ascetic-practice) results including a specific temple event (臨済寺 修行体験 with price/access) and forest ninja experience — both are touristy. Constraint 'not touristy' is ignored. Search_area has no 'crowd_band=low' filter applied, so busy tourist sites mix in.
missing_data: Remoter temple-stay (shukubo) options, mountain hermitage retreats, quiet forest dojo — crowd_band=low filtering absent.
improvement_hint: Apply crowd_band=low filter or add a 'non-touristy' intent class that boosts low-prominence, low-crowd results over event-page hits.

reasoning: Excellent infeasibility response: not_available block clearly explains auroras are not viable in Japan with Japanese + English reasons, offers dark_sky/observatory alt_kinds, and returns relevant alternatives. One caveat: response is in English though query was Spanish (RULE D) — constraint_handling -1. Otherwise exemplary handling.
missing_data: Spanish-language reason_es field would fully satisfy RULE D.
improvement_hint: Add reason_es (and other language variants) to the not_available block so multilingual infeasibility messages reach users in their query language.

reasoning: RULE A: wrong tool. Query asks for 古民家 (100-year-old traditional house) lodging. Tool called get_hotels(prefecture=Gifu) which returns onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai) and generic OSM hotels — none tagged as kominka or historic building. Authoritative data but wrong entity class entirely. Gifu has famous kominka in Shirakawa-go but none surface here.
missing_data: Kominka-conversion guesthouses, machiya stays, Shirakawa-go farmhouse lodging (Gassho-zukuri).
improvement_hint: Add kominka lodging_type tag to hotels and route 'old house' / 古民家 queries to filter by that type; or surface search_area with q=古民家 宿泊.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Thai but lang=th is passed — no Thai name/description fields visible. More critically, query asks for off-the-beaten-path cherry blossom spots away from tourists. Response returns generic 桜 query results dominated by famous sites (Himeji Castle, Kenroku-en) with high tourist volumes — opposite of intent. Reasoning failure on 'uncrowded' constraint.
missing_data: Low-prominence sakura spots (crowd_band=low), sakura_meisho_100 entries in less-visited prefectures.
improvement_hint: Detect '穴場/hidden' modifier in queries and boost crowd_band=low + lower prominence_score results while suppressing famous tourist sites.

reasoning: Query asks for dramatic photography landscapes; 絶景 search returns valid but mis-ranked results. Top results are DMO editorial pages (feature articles) rather than specific named landscape spots. The article-page blobs dominate with repeated text (tourist guide articles) that bury actual spot names. Ranking failure: spot entities should rank above scrape-page feature articles.
missing_data: Named dramatic landscape spots (Tottori Dunes, Kegon Falls, Nachi Falls, Shiretoko) with coordinates.
improvement_hint: Downrank dmo_website feature-article results in favor of wikidata attraction + municipal spot entities for specificity-seeking queries.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Arabic (ar) but response shows no Arabic name/description fields — all content is Japanese. Constraint_handling -1 applied. Content itself is relevant (outdoor hot spring spots, onsen facilities) and authoritative. The 'not in hotels' constraint is partially addressed — some entries are public day-use baths rather than hotel facilities.
missing_data: Arabic language fields; more prominent outdoor/wild onsen like Kurokawa or Nyuto Onsen with coordinates.
improvement_hint: Populate Arabic translation fields for onsen records; add 'outdoor' or 'rotenburo' kind tag to distinguish wild/public baths from hotel facilities.

reasoning: Query asks to swim with wild dolphins in Japan. イルカ search returns irrelevant results: top result is Kyoto tourism ambassador page (mentions China tea association), second is Noto aquarium (captive dolphins in shows). No wild dolphin swimming spots returned. The key venue (Mikurajima, Amami Oshima) is entirely absent. This is a retrieval failure for a feasible but niche activity.
missing_data: Mikurajima (Tokyo) and Amami Oshima wild dolphin swimming — the two main sites in Japan for this activity.
improvement_hint: Add wild_dolphin_swimming kind tag to Mikurajima and similar venues; differentiate from aquarium/captive dolphin content.

reasoning: RULE A: wrong tool (get_japan_heritage instead of search_area or get_spots with preservation_district filter). Query asks for traditional architecture untouched by modernization — 重伝建 (Preservation Districts) are the canonical answer. Response returns Japan Heritage stories, which overlap but are a different program. Authoritative data (RULE C) but systematically misses 重伝建地区 category.
missing_data: 重伝建 (Important Preservation Districts): Shirakawa-go, Gion Higashiyama, Tsumago-juku etc. — the direct answer.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional architecture / preservation district' queries to search_area with preservation_district kind filter or add 重伝建 as a first-class tool parameter.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Vietnamese but response has no Vietnamese fields (vi) — constraint_handling -1. Otherwise excellent: National Ainu Museum, Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Kawamura Kaneto Museum all returned with correct coordinates and Hokkaido prefecture — directly addresses the query. Query was geographically specific (Hokkaido) and results are appropriately Hokkaido-filtered.
missing_data: Vietnamese name/description fields; Upopoy (ウポポイ) cultural complex reference.

reasoning: Strong response: top results are directly on-topic (Usuki Stone Buddhas — National Treasure with coordinates, Oya-dera with coordinates, Komakasa Magaibutsu). Multiple prefectures covered (Oita, Tochigi, Shiga, Kagoshima). Authoritative heritage designations. One caveat: several entries lack name_en translation. No cluster block but direct keyword match is strong.
missing_data: English names for several entries (狛坂磨崖仏, 大岩弘法院磨崖仏); visiting hours/access info.
improvement_hint: Ensure English translation for all magaibutsu records; add access/transport notes from get_transport.

reasoning: Query asks for day-trip volcano hikes FROM TOKYO. Top result is Mount Iwate (Iwate Prefecture — ~4h from Tokyo, not day-trip feasible). Lake Towada and Mount Unzen appear — also not day-trip from Tokyo. Mount Fuji is present but not highlighted. The key Tokyo day-trip volcanos (Nikko/Nantai, Izu Oshima, Hakone Owakudani) are absent or buried. Feasibility constraint 'from Tokyo' is ignored.
missing_data: Hakone Owakudani, Izu Oshima, Mount Nantai — all day-trip viable from Tokyo. Distance/transport time from Tokyo.
improvement_hint: Add proximity-to-major-hub filter; surface travel_time or distance metadata so agents can filter day-trip feasibility.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Indonesian (id), lang=id passed, but no Indonesian fields visible — however this is minor. More critically, query asks for 'freshly made tofu AT a temple.' Response returns tofu GI products, a tofu recipe, regional tofu food culture articles — no temple-tofu experience venues (Kyoto shojin ryori, Nanzen-ji tofu restaurants, Koya-san temple tofu) are returned. Reasoning failure: food product corpus matched, experiential temple venue missed.

reasoning: Query asks for 'fishing villages frozen in time' — the intent is historic/preserved fishing villages (例: Ine no Funaya, Tomonoura, Kujukushima). Response returns modern active fishing ports (漁港): Itoman Harbor operational port, seafood market guide, access restrictions at Shirahama fishing harbor. These are current working ports, not historically preserved villages. Reasoning failure on 'frozen in time' preservation intent.
missing_data: Ine no Funaya (Kyoto), Tomonoura (Hiroshima), historic preserved fishing villages with 重伝建 or Japan Heritage designation.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_village kind-class match routing to preservation_district and Japan Heritage entries rather than active working-port records.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional washi paper-making experience from a master. get_local_specialty with craft filter returns 231 craft records. Top results are textiles (Nibutani Attus, Oitama Tsumugi, Ugo Shina Cloth) — no washi records in the visible portion. Washi is present in METI densan corpus (Echizen Washi, Mino Washi etc.) but buried or absent from top results. Coverage failure for the specific craft requested.

reasoning: Excellent result for jidaigeki/period-drama scenery query. Top results are directly relevant: Tsumago-juku (must_see, Important Preservation District, coordinates), Narai-juku, Seki-juku — all shukuba post-towns with preservation_district designation. Detailed descriptive spot entries add authentic atmosphere (water stations, old merchants). Strong specificity with named preservation districts.
missing_data: Kyoto Higashiyama preservation districts, Gion Shirakawa; access tips.

reasoning: Query asks for uncrowded beaches. Response returns beaches with coordinates and metadata but crowd_band is 'medium' for top results (Naminoue, Hamasaka, Sunayama) — not low-crowd. The constraint 'not crowded with tourists' is not filtered by crowd_band. Multiple Okinawa beaches returned which are popular tourist destinations, opposite of intent.
missing_data: Low-crowd-band beach entries from less-visited prefectures (Akita, Shimane, Kochi coastline).

reasoning: Query asks for old-school izakaya with locals. 横丁 search returns relevant yokocho entries (Haikara't Yokocho, etc.) but top result is a park (横丁公園 in Tokyo), and second result is a Saga tourism guide in Chinese — both are irrelevant noise. reasoning: RULE D: query is German (de), lang=de passed, but no German name/description fields returned. Constraint_handling -1. reasoning: Good response for firefly viewing. Top result is Hotaruika (firefly squid) — different species, slightly misleading ranking but not wrong (has correct heritage designations). Hotaru-no-sato park and national monument firefly habitats follow. Specific venue (Toyoda Hotaru Museum with website) and detailed Yamaguchi hotaru-bune river experience included with dates. Seasonal info present. One caveat: ホタルイカ vs ホタル distinction slightly confusing for the agent.
missing_data: Viewing season dates (May-July) for most entries; specific best-viewing locations beyond the museum.

reasoning: RULE D: query is Russian (ru), lang=ru passed, but no Russian name/description fields visible in results. Constraint_handling -1. Content is very relevant: top result is Izumi crane migration grounds (Kagoshima) with precise description of 10,000 winter cranes Oct-March, correct coordinates, Ramsar and Special Natural Monument designations. Kushiro Shitsugen National Park (Red-crowned crane habitat) also present. Strong factual match despite language gap.
